scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the live AssemblyAI API reference (assemblyai.com/docs/api-reference/transcripts/*). AssemblyAI's real documented surface is small and entirely transcript-centric: one list endpoint, one detail endpoint, three per-transcript sub-resource reads (sentences/paragraphs/word-search), one text-format sub-resource (subtitles, not JSON), one create endpoint, one delete endpoint, and the separate LeMUR generative-task product. Every JSON-shaped GET is now covered: the 4 legacy-parity list-backed streams, a fan_out transcript_detail stream (GET /v2/transcript/{id}, ids listed from the transcript list endpoint), fan_out transcript_sentence_items/transcript_paragraph_items streams (flattening each transcript's sentence/paragraph array into its own row, stamped with transcript_id), and a fan_out transcript_word_search_matches stream (GET /v2/transcript/{id}/word-search, keyword list from config.word_search_terms). create_transcript is now a write action (POST /v2/transcript, audio_url required). Real-time/streaming transcription (a separate WebSocket protocol, not REST) and LeMUR (a generative-summarization task over already-transcribed text, not a transcript-domain CRUD resource) remain out of scope; subtitles and redacted audio remain excluded for real, closed reasons below (not a blanket bucket) — see Known limits.

excluded.reason: returns a raw text/plain SRT or VTT caption document, not a JSON record; the engine's declarative read path decodes only JSON response bodies (connsdk.RecordsAt), so this endpoint has no schema-shaped record to project
returns a redacted audio/wav or audio/mp3 binary media stream, not a JSON record
permanently deletes the transcript's text/pii and associated data; not a syncable read or a safe autonomous mutation
LeMUR is a generative-summarization/QA task over already-transcribed text (an LLM prompt-completion product), not a transcript-domain CRUD resource; no transcript-shaped record to read or write
purges a LeMUR request's stored data; out of scope alongside the rest of the LeMUR product surface
real-time streaming transcription is a stateful WebSocket protocol (audio frames in, partial/final transcript events out), not a REST resource this engine's declarative HTTP dialect can express
